params.description: Show the diff from the bug patch

final_text:
Confirmed — `TooManyRedirects` is imported (line 28) and `self.max_redirects` is still set (line 432) and documented (line 429), but the actual enforcement check was the only place it was used and it has been removed.

```json
[
  {
    "title": "Redirect loop guard removed — infinite redirect loop possible",
    "category": "reliability",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.99,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 188,
    "end_line": 189,
    "evidence": [
      "The diff removes the only check: `if len(resp.history) >= self.max_redirects: raise TooManyRedirects(...)`",
      "The `while url:` loop at line 177 now has no upper-bound guard on iterations",
      "`self.max_redirects` is still set to DEFAULT_REDIRECT_LIMIT (30) at line 432 but is never read",
      "`TooManyRedirects` is still imported at line 28 but is never raised"
    ],
    "risk": "Any HTTP response chain that redirects more than expected (e.g., a misconfigured server returning 301/302 in a cycle) will cause the client to loop forever, hanging the calling thread, exhausting memory as `hist` grows unboundedly, and consuming network/server resources. This is also a denial-of-service vector: a malicious server can trap any requests client in an infinite loop.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the removed guard immediately after the `resp.raw.read(decode_content=False)` except block (before `resp.close()`): \n\n            if len(resp.history) >= self.max_redirects:\n                raise TooManyRedirects(\n                    f\"Exceeded {self.max_redirects} redirects.\", response=resp\n                )"
  },
  {
    "title": "Removal of redirect limit is a denial-of-service vulnerability",
    "category": "security",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.97,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 188,
    "end_line": 189,
    "evidence": [
      "Without the max_redirects check, a malicious server can return an endless chain of 3xx redirects",
      "The `while url:` loop at line 177 follows every redirect unconditionally",
      "Each iteration appends to `hist` (line 182) so memory grows without bound",
      "Each iteration issues a new HTTP request via `self.send()` (line 262)"
    ],
    "risk": "A malicious or compromised server can force any application using the requests library into an infinite loop of HTTP requests, causing CPU/memory exhaustion (DoS) on the client side and potentially amplifying traffic toward arbitrary redirect targets.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the `TooManyRedirects` check that was removed. The check should be placed before `resp.close()` at the original location (after consuming the response body), comparing `len(resp.history)` against `self.max_redirects`."
  }
]
```
